aerial_assaulter.name: Aerial Assaulter
description: ## Aerial Assaulter
‹SOURCE Blood of the Ancients/27›

As staunch believers in the value of higher ground, the Shory developed special fighting styles that capitalize on fighting from above. While many dismiss such techniques as a niche tactic, aerial assaulters leap to great heights and create higher ground where there is none.
::cskill{gain=acro~fly lose=kd~ride~swim}

::ab[Aerial Expertise (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="An aerial assaulter gains a +2 bonus on Fly checks. Additionally, whenever he is affected by a spell or ability that grants him a fly speed, he treats that spell or ability as though its caster level were 2 higher for the purposes of determining its effects." increment="The bonus on Fly checks~2~4~4~2" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Take the High Ground (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost flavor="An aerial assaulter can leap above his foes to gain an advantage in combat." move="He can attempt an Acrobatics check with the DC equal to the CMD of an adjacent enemy. If he succeeds, the aerial assaulter is treated as if he had ‹rule/higher ground› against that enemy until the beginning of his next turn." imp4="An aerial assaulter increases his bonus from higher ground by an amount equal to ‹misc/one-fourth› of his fighter level." imp12="An aerial assaulter can use this ability as a @HLswift_action." replace="Bonus feats gained at 2nd and 12th level"}

::ab[Aerial Dodge (Ex)]{l=19 icon=def flavor="An aerial assaulter can perform an aerial dodge that allows him to evade attacks while flying." immediate="When an opponent attempts an attack against the aerial assaulter, he can attempt a Fly check with a DC equal to the result of the attacker's attack roll. If he succeeds, he can take a 5-foot step and gain a dodge bonus to his AC equal to his Dexterity modifier. This movement doesn't negate the attack if the aerial assaulter remains in range of the attacking enemy's reach at the end of the 5-foot step. If the Fly check fails, the aerial assaulter does not move, does not gain an AC bonus, and provokes an attack of opportunity from each creature that threatens him, including the initial attacker." special="The aerial assaulter must be flying to use this ability." replace="Armor mastery"}

::ab[High Ground Mastery (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost-def ability="Whenever an aerial assaulter threatens a critical hit with an attack made from higher ground, he automatically confirms the critical hit, and the weapon's damage multiplier is increased by 1." order=ap passive="All bull rush, dirty trick, drag, grapple, reposition, and trip combat maneuver checks against the aerial assaulter fail when attempted by opponents on lower ground than he is." replace="Weapon mastery"}

airborne_ambusher.name: Airborne Ambusher
description: ## Airborne Ambusher
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/201›
Driven by suspicion and hatred, ‹race/strix› doggedly guard their territories, making deadly use of their flight. Using swift strikes from above, strix plummet onto their foes with lethal force.
::prereq{r=Strix}
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down info="An airborne ambusher is not proficient with heavy armor or tower shields."}
::cskill{gain=fly lose=climb}
::ab[Combat Flyer (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost choice="An airborne ambusher may use his fighter bonus feats to select ‹feat/Flyby Attack› and ‹feat/Hover›." replace=Bravery}

::ab[Aerobatics (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def passive="An airborne ambusher may make a Fly check instead of an Acrobatics check to move through a threatened area or an enemy's space." replace="Weapon training 1"}

::ab[Flying Dodger (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def passive="When an airborne ambusher flies at least half its fly speed on its turn, it gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC for 1 round." imp11="The bonus becomes +2." imp17="The bonus increases to +4." replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Plummeting Charge (Ex)]{l=13 icon=boost passive="If an airborne ambusher flies at least half its fly speed as part of a ‹rule/charge›, it gains a +2 racial bonus on the attack roll (in addition to the normal charge bonus) and a +4 bonus on its critical confirmation roll." imp17="These bonuses improve to +4 and +6." replace="Weapon training 3 and 4"}

aldori_defender.name: Aldori Defender
description: ## Aldori Defender
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/22;Inner Sea Primer/24›

The Aldori swordlords of Brevoy are among the deadliest and most feared fighters of the Inner Sea. They have spent long years mastering the Aldori dueling sword, pitting themselves against other dueling swords and all manner of weaponry besides. Their speed and reflexes weave a net of impenetrable steel around them, from which they strike and harry their unfortunate opponents. The most common form of Aldori dueling (and arguably the easiest of the techniques to master) focuses on avoiding damage and disarming foes; these swordlords prefer to wear light or no armor, trusting their skill for protection. This archetype focuses on such techniques - characters seeking a more offensive method of focusing training on the Aldori dueling sword should consider the ‹pclass/Aldori swordlord› prestige class. However, this archetype also synergizes well with the powers and skills the prestige class offers.

@HL[Important:] The following benefits apply only when an Aldori defender is wielding an ‹eq-weapon/Aldori dueling sword› and carrying nothing in his other hand. An Aldori defender fights with his sword wielded with both hands only in moments of desperation.

::ab[Defensive Parry (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="When an Aldori defender makes a ‹rule/full attack› with an Aldori dueling sword, he gains a +1 shield bonus to his AC against melee attacks until the beginning of his next turn." imp7="This bonus increases to +2." imp11="The bonus becomes +3." imp15="The bonus increases to +4." replace="Armor training"}

::ab[Disarming Strike (Ex)]{l=6 icon=boost passive="When an Aldori defender successfully ‹rule/disarm«s› an opponent using an Aldori dueling sword, he also deals normal damage to the target, but without the normal Strength bonus to damage." replace="6th level fighter bonus feat"}

::ab[Steel Net (Ex)]{l=8 icon=boost-def flavor="An Aldori defender can create a blazing wall of steel to defend himself." fullround="When ‹misc/fighting defensively› as a full-round action with an Aldori dueling sword, the Aldori defender's penalties on all attacks in a round are reduced by 2, and his dodge bonus to AC increases by 2 for the same round." replace="8th level fighter bonus feat"}

::ab[Counterattack (Ex)]{l=10 icon=power ability="Once per round, an Aldori defender can make an attack of opportunity against an opponent who hits him with a melee attack (regardless of how many attacks of opportunity the Aldori defender can otherwise make), so long as the attacking creature is within the his reach." replace="10th level fighter bonus feat"}

@HL[Suggested Feats:] Although this archetype replaces some of the bonus feats that a fighter would normally have access to, Aldori defenders still have several opportunities to gain feats. The following feats are the most thematically appropriate for an Aldori defender, but the archetype does not require any of them: ‹feat/Aldori Dueling Mastery›, ‹feat/Combat Expertise›, ‹feat/Dazzling Display›, ‹feat/Exotic Weapon Proficiency› (Aldori dueling sword), ‹feat/Improved Disarm›, ‹feat/Improved Feint›, ‹feat/Improved Initiative›, ‹feat/Lunge›, ‹feat/Quick Draw›, ‹feat/Weapon Finesse›, ‹feat/Weapon Focus› (Aldori dueling sword), ‹feat/Weapon Specialization› (Aldori dueling sword).

aquanaut.name: Aquanaut
description: ## Aquanaut
‹SOURCE Aquatic Adventures/51›
Aquanaut fighters master the movement of water, using its flow to their advantage in combat.
::ab[Armor Proficiency]{icon=down info="An aquanaut is proficient with light and medium armor, but not with shields." alter="Armor proficiency"}

::ab[Piercing Focus]{icon=boost ability="If an aquanaut takes a fighter bonus feat that normally requires selecting a single weapon (except weapon proficiency feats), he can instead apply the feat to all melee piercing weapons with which he is already proficient and that meet all other prerequisites for that feat." special="This ability counts as having the feat for each such individual weapon for the purpose of meeting prerequisites. This ability also applies to the *weapon mastery* class feature." alter="Bonus feats~weapon mastery"}

::ab[Tidal Celerity]{l=2 icon=def passive="An aquanaut gains a +1 bonus on Reflex saves and saving throws against effects that would immobilize or paralyze him." increment="The bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Aquadynamic Paragon]{l=3 icon=power-def order=af ability="An aquanaut gains a swim speed equal to his unmodified base land speed (if he has a racial swim speed, he instead increases it by 10 feet)." free="He can choose to have a neutral ‹rule/buoyancy› once per turn." imp7="He can choose any of the five states of buoyancy once per turn as a @HLfree_action, and can take his buoyancy movement as part of that free action instead of at the end of his turn without becoming ‹rule/off-balance› and without requiring a Swim check; additionally, he can choose to double or halve how far a ‹rule/current› moves him." imp11="Whenever he takes a *move, double move, withdraw,* or *run* action underwater, he can leave a current behind him in the squares along his path; this current has a speed of 10 feet in the direction of his movement (if his movement was circuitous, so is the current) and lasts until the beginning of his next turn." imp15="As a @HLfull-round_action, an aquanaut can spin ferociously underwater, creating a whirlpool around himself with a spinning force that drags all creatures within 120 feet of him 30 feet closer to him. Creatures closer than 30 feet spend the 30 feet of movement circling around the aquanaut in a single direction of his choice. The whirlpool isn't fast enough to deal damage, but the aquanaut is unaffected by his own whirlpool, allowing him to make attacks of opportunity against creatures moving in it." imp19="The aquanaut learns to use the water around him to soften blows, gaining DR 5/- while underwater." replace="Armor training~armor mastery"}

::ab[Underwater Training]{l=5 icon=boost choice="An aquanaut chooses bludgeoning, slashing, or piercing melee weapons. If he chooses piercing melee weapons, he gains a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls with those weapons when attacking underwater. If he chooses bludgeoning or slashing melee weapons, he can ignore ‹rule/the usual underwater penalties»<physical_attacks_underwater› for attacking with those weapons." imp9="He can choose another type of melee weapon, applying this ability's initial benefit to his new choice and gaining an additional +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls to his previously chosen weapon type." imp13="He gains the benefits with his remaining type of melee weapon, and gains another additional +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls to his previously chosen weapon types." imp17="He gains an additional +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls with all three types." replace="Weapon training"}

archer.name: Archer
description: ## Archer
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/104›

The archer is dedicated to the careful mastery of the bow, perfecting his skills with years of practice honed day after day on ranges and hunting for game, or else on the battlefield, raining destruction down on the enemy lines.

::ab[Hawkeye (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="An archer gains a bonus on Perception checks equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fourth› his fighter level. The range increment for any bow he uses increases by 5 feet, plus an additional 5 feet for every four fighter levels he has." replace=Bravery}

::ab[Trick Shot (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power choice="An archer can choose one of the following combat maneuvers or actions: ‹rule/disarm›, ‹rule/feint›, or ‹rule/sunder›. He can perform this action with a bow against any target within 30 feet, with a -4 penalty to his CMB." imp7="He can choose a second option to receive this same benefit." imp11="He can choose a third trick shot option. Those options expand to include ‹rule/bull rush›, ‹rule/grapple›, and ‹rule/trip›. A target grappled by an arrow can break free by destroying the archer's arrow (hardness 5, hit points 1, break DC 13) or with an Escape Artist or CMB check (against the archer's CMD -4)." imp15="He can choose a fourth trick shot." imp19="He can choose a fifth trick shot." replace="Armor training 1, 2, 3, and 4" special="These maneuvers use up arrows as normal."}
::ab[Expert Archer (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="An archer gains a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls with bows." increment="This bonus~5~4" replace="Weapon training 1"}
::ab[Safe Shot (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def passive="An archer does not provoke attacks of opportunity when making ranged attacks with a bow." replace="Weapon training 2"}
::ab[Evasive Archer (Ex)]{l=13 icon=def passive="An archer gains a +2 dodge bonus to AC against ranged attacks." imp17="This bonus becomes +4." replace="Weapon training 3"}

::ab[Volley (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power fullround="An archer can make a single bow attack at his highest base attack bonus against any number of creatures in a 15-foot-radius ‹misc/burst›, making separate attack and damage rolls for each creature." replace="Weapon training 4"}

::ab[Ranged Defense (Ex)]{l=19 icon=def passive="An archer gains DR 5/- against ranged attacks." order=pi immediate="He can catch an arrow fired at him and shoot it any target he chooses, as if he had the ‹feat/Snatch Arrows› feat." replace="Armor mastery"}
::ab[Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{icon=info info="An archer must choose a type of bow." alter="Weapon mastery"}

armiger.name: Armiger
description: ## Armiger
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/94›

Until would-be ‹rule/Hellknights› take the trial referred to simply as the Test, in which they enter single combat with a conjured devil and either emerge victorious or die, they are known as armigers. While this is a blanket term for all Hellknights-in-training (including those training to become ‹pclass/hellknight_>signifer«s›), it also applies specifically to fighters who focus their training to become Hellknights. Despite their lack of recognized rank, armigers are skilled and deadly warriors.

Most armigers live and train at their order's citadel, working toward the day when they become full-fledged Hellknights. These armigers follow a brutally strict routine; they spend hours honing their combat skills against other trainees, obsessively studying in the citadel's libraries, or memorizing passages of the Hellknight philosophy known as the Measure and the Chain. At regular intervals, squads of armigers emerge from their citadels to patrol nearby cities or frontier lands, working to enforce Hellknight law and their order's edicts.

On occasion, some armigers travel farther from their citadels, searching out those who mock the law and bringing them to justice by any means necessary. While a few do so as squires to full-fledged Hellknights, others strike out on their own or join bands of adventurers. To make up for the broken routine of adventuring life and the frequent compromises necessary in the presence of such allies, such roaming armigers are expected to perform frequent reckonings. These painful acts of mortification, such as scourging oneself or drinking boiling water, keep the would-be Hellknight disciplined and focused on the strictures of the Measure and the Chain.

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power info="When selecting fighter bonus feats, the armiger can select any feats whose names include the word &quot&Hellknight&quot& as though they were combat feats." alter="Bonus feats"}

::ab[Hellknight Order]{l=1 icon=info choice="An armiger must select which Hellknight order she intends to join. If the armiger later gains levels in a Hellknight prestige class, she must select that same order."}

::ab[Studious Squire]{icon=skill-boost info="An armiger gains 2 additional skill ranks at each level. These ranks must be put into Intimidate and Knowledge (planes). In addition, the armiger treats ‹skill/Knowledge (local)› (INT) and ‹skill/Knowledge (planes)› (INT) as class skills." alter="Class skills~skill points per level" replace="Bonus feats gained at 1st and 10th levels"}

::ab[Ardent (Su)]{l=2 icon=def flavor="Armigers are difficult to sway from their beliefs." passive="An armiger gains a +1 bonus on Will saves against charm effects." order=pa ability="Once per day, if an armiger is forced to take an action that is diametrically opposed to her order's beliefs and values while under the influence of a charm or compulsion effect, she can immediately attempt a Will save against the effect's DC to resist acting out that order. Success does not remove the existing charm or compulsion effect, but does allow the armiger to resist betraying her beliefs." increment="The Will save bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

armor_master.name: Armor Master
description: ## Armor Master
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/44›

All fighters have two main tools of their trade: weapons and armor. While many fighters hone their weapon skills to a point of inescapable grace and lethality, there are those who live under the maxim that a good offense can be accomplished though an impenetrable defense. To these fighters, proper use of armor and shields ensures that they can fight another day, and that the frustration of enemies who can't seem to crack an armor master's superior defenses is just the first symptom of his foes' eventual defeat.

::ab[Deflective Shield (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def flavor="An armor master specializes in using his shield to deflect attacks." passive="He gains a +1 bonus to his touch AC, and this bonus increases for every four levels beyond 2nd (+2 at 6th level, +3 at 10th level, +4 at 14th level, and finally +5 at 18th level); however, this bonus cannot exceed the sum of the armor and enhancement bonus to AC provided by the shield that the armor master is currently carrying." replace=Bravery}

::ab[Armored Defense (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def passive="An armor master gains DR 1/- when wearing light armor, DR 2/- when wearing medium armor, and DR 3/- when wearing heavy armor." imp19="This damage reduction increases to DR 4/- when wearing light armor, DR 8/- when wearing medium armor, and DR 12/- when wearing heavy armor." special="This damage reduction stacks with that provided by ‹eq-material/adamantine› armor, but not with other forms of damage reduction. This damage reduction does not apply if the armor master is stunned, unconscious, or helpless." replace="Weapon training 1 and 3~armor mastery"}

::ab[Fortification (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def flavor="An armor master can use his armor to shield critical areas from injury." passive="He treats any armor he wears as if it had the ‹magic-enh/light fortification› special ability." imp13="His armor gains the ‹magic-enh/moderate fortification› special ability." special="This fortification does not stack in any way with armor that has these special abilities. In these cases the armor master takes the better of the two fortifications." replace="Weapon training 2 and 4"}

::ab[Indestructible (Ex)]{l=20 icon=def passive="An armor master gains complete immunity to critical hits and sneak attacks while he is wearing armor. In addition, unless his armor has the ‹misc/fragile› armor quality, it cannot be ‹rule/sunder«ed› while he is wearing it." replace="Weapon mastery"}

brawler.name: Brawler
description: ## Brawler
>***Note:** This is a fighter archetype, and has no relation to the ‹class/brawler› hybrid class.*
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/44›

All melee is up close and personal, but some warriors bring it as close as they can get. Brawlers can be found anywhere, among all races and societies. A brawler could be hired muscle in a tavern, a local crime syndicate enforcer, or a hotheaded recruit among the ranks of a baron's guard. Often brawlers' in-your-face attitudes are as powerful as their tactics.

::ab[Close Control (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-def flavor="A brawler becomes skilled at forcefully moving his opponent around the battlefield." passive="The brawler gains a +1 bonus on ‹rule/bull rush›, ‹rule/drag›, and ‹rule/reposition› combat maneuver checks. The brawler also gains a +1 bonus to CMD when attacked with the bull rush, drag, and reposition maneuvers." increment="p!These bonuses~2~4" replace="Armor training 1"}

::ab[Close Combatant (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A brawler gains a +1 bonus on attack rolls and a +3 bonus on damage rolls with weapons in the close ‹ability/weapon group<s›." imp7="These bonuses increase to +2 and +4, respectively." imp11="The bonuses become +3 and +5." imp15="These bonuses increase to +4 and +6." imp19="The bonuses become +5 on attack rolls and +7 on damage rolls." replace="Weapon training 1 and 2"}

::ab[Menacing Stance (Ex)]{l=7 icon=lower flavor="A brawler constantly harries and distracts his enemies." passive="While adjacent to the brawler, enemies take a -1 penalty on attack rolls and a -4 penalty on ‹rule/concentration› checks." imp11="These penalties increase to -2 and -5, respectively." imp15="The penalties become -3 and -6." imp19="These penalties increase to -4 on attack rolls and -7 on concentration checks." special="Creatures do not take these penalties if the brawler is dazed, helpless, staggered, stunned, or unconscious." replace="Armor training 2, 3, and 4~armor mastery"}

::ab[No Escape (Ex)]{l=9 icon=power-lower passive="Taking ‹rule/a 5-foot step› out of the area of a brawler's *menacing stance* or moving out of the area of a brawler's *menacing stance* with a ‹rule/withdraw› action provokes an attack of opportunity from the brawler." replace="Weapon training 3 and 4"}

::ab[Stand Still]{l=13 icon=power-boost ability="A brawler gains ‹feat/Stand Still› as a bonus feat, even if he does not have the ‹feat/Combat Reflexes› feat. If the brawler already has the Stand Still feat, he can take any other ‹main/combat feat› instead." order=ap passive="He gains a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› his fighter level on combat maneuver checks when using the Stand Still feat."}
::ab[Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{icon=info choice="A brawler must select a close weapon for this ability." alter="Weapon mastery"}

cad.name: Cad
description: ## Cad
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/45›

In combat, most fighters have some sort of code of honor. Some believe that one should not kick enemies when they are down, or should limit use of other such dirty tricks to the most dire of circumstances. The cad places no such limitations on himself. Battles are for winning, and anything that gives the cad the upper hand against his enemies is a legitimate tactic. No trick is too treacherous if it leads to victory.
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down info="A cad is not proficient with medium armor, heavy armor, or tower shields."}
::cskill{gain=acro~bluff~ea~soh~stl}

::ab[Dirty Maneuvers (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-def flavor="A cad becomes skilled at deceiving and discomfiting his opponents." passive="The cad gains a +1 bonus on ‹rule/disarm›, ‹rule/dirty trick›, and ‹rule/steal› combat maneuver checks. The cad also gains a +1 bonus to CMD when attacked with the disarm, dirty trick, and steal combat maneuvers." increment="p!These bonuses~2~4" replace=Bravery}
::ab[Catch Off-Guard]{l=3 icon=power ability="The cad gains the ‹feat/Catch Off-Guard› feat." replace="Armor training 1"}

::ab[Payback (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="A cad gains a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls against any creature that has attacked the cad since the beginning of his last turn." increment="This bonus~5~4" replace="Weapon training 1, 3, and 4"}

::ab[Deadly Surprise (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power ability="When a cad hits an opponent that is denied its Dexterity bonus to AC against him with a weapon or unarmed attack, he may attempt a dirty trick combat maneuver as an @HLimmediate_action as part of the attack." replace="Armor training 2"}

::ab[Razor-Sharp Chair Leg (Ex)]{l=9 icon=power-boost swift="A cad may alter the type of damage dealt by an ‹misc/improvised weapon› to bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage." order=wp passive="The cad has a critical threat range of 19-20/&times&2 with any improvised melee weapon." replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Craven Combatant (Ex)]{l=11 icon=def passive="When ‹misc/fighting defensively› or using ‹feat/Combat Expertise› or ‹misc/total defense›, a cad cannot be ‹rule/flanked› except by a rogue or ninja whose level is four or more higher than the cad's fighter level." replace="Armor training 3"}

::ab[Sweeping Prank (Ex)]{l=13 icon=power standard="A cad can use a dirty trick maneuver against any two adjacent opponents that he can reach, making a separate combat maneuver check against each opponent. He must use the same dirty trick maneuver against each opponent." imp17="The cad can use this ability as a @HLfull-round_action to attempt a dirty trick maneuver against a number of enemies equal to 2 + his Dexterity bonus (if any)." replace="Weapon training 3 and 4"}
::ab[Treacherous Blow (Ex)]{l=15 icon=power immediate="When a cad confirms a critical hit, he can attempt a dirty trick combat maneuver as part of that attack." replace="Armor training 4"}

::ab[Ultimate Payback (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost passive="Any critical threats a cad makes against an opponent that has attacked him since the beginning of his last turn are automatically confirmed." replace="Weapon mastery"}

calistrian_hunter.name: Calistrian Hunter
description: ## Calistrian Hunter
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/36›

Not every ‹faith/Calistria«n› who dedicates himself to the goddess can channel divine magic to track down those who deserve punishment. Like a bounty hunter following his own rules, a Calistrian hunter tracks down elusive prey using his wits and delivers long-overdue retribution while easily ignoring his own injuries thanks to the joy he receives from a job well done.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-down info="A Calistrian hunter is proficient with all simple and martial weapons, plus the ‹eq-weapon/whip›. In addition, the hunter is proficient with light and medium armor as well as shields (except tower shields)." replace="Armor and weapon proficiencies"}
::cskill{gain=diplo~kl~per lose=kd~ke}

::ab[Tenacious Tracker (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A Calistrian hunter gains a +1 bonus on Diplomacy checks to gather information and on Survival checks made to identify or follow tracks." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Vengeance (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost-lower ability="A Calistrian hunter deals 1d4 points of ‹rule/bleed› damage when he damages a creature that has damaged him since the beginning of his last turn. Whenever a creature takes bleed damage from this effect, it also takes a -1 penalty on attack rolls, weapon damage rolls, saving throws, skill checks, and ability checks for 1 round. This penalty is a ‹spelldef/pain› effect and does not stack with the effects of the ‹misc/sickened› condition." imp9="The bleed damage becomes 1d6." imp13="The bleed increases to 1d8." imp17="The bleed damage increases to 2d6." replace="Weapon training 1"}

::ab[Savor the Sting (Ex)]{l=9 icon=boost passive="Whenever a target takes bleed damage from a Calistrian hunter's *vengeance* ability, the Calistrian hunter gains an equal number of ‹rule/temporary hit points›. These temporary hit points last for 1 minute and do not stack with each other." replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Swift Revenge (Ex)]{l=13 icon=power ability="A Calistrian hunter gains the quarry class feature; this functions like the ‹class/ranger› ability of the same name, but the target must be one that has damaged the Calistrian hunter in the past hour." replace="Weapon training 3"}

::ab[Perceived Wrongs (Ex)]{l=17 icon=boost ability="A Calistrian hunter can, once per day, use his *vengeance* ability against any creature - even one that has not harmed him. If the target is also one that has damaged him since the beginning of his last turn, the bleed damage increases to 3d6 and the target is sickened for as long as the bleed effect continues." replace="Weapon training 4"}

::ab[Certain Revenge (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power ability="A Calistrian hunter gains the *improved quarry* class feature; this functions like the ranger ability of the same name, but the target must have attacked him in the past day. Whenever he uses his *vengeance* ability against his quarry, the bleed damage increases by 2 per bleed damage die." replace="Weapon mastery"}

cavern_sniper.name: Cavern Sniper
description: ## Cavern Sniper
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/104›

Perfectly at home in the darkness, the cavern sniper capitalizes on stealth and ranged attacks imbued with his spell-like abilities to harass his opponents. The cavern sniper focuses on surprise, his innate magical abilities, and poison to take down unwary foes. The cavern sniper has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Drow}
::cskill{gain=stl lose=intm}

::ab[Imbued Shot (Su)]{l=1 icon=magic swift="The cavern sniper gains the ability to imbue his arrows or bolts with the effect of one of his drow ‹spell/faerie fire›, ‹spell/darkness›, or ‹spell/deeper darkness› spell-like abilities (provided he has access to the ability). When such an arrow or bolt is fired, the spell's area is centered where the arrow or bolt lands. If the target of the attack has a space larger than 5 feet, the cavern sniper can choose which square of the creature's space is the center of the spell-like ability's effect, as long as that square is within line of sight of the cavern sniper. The cavern sniper can instead choose to target a single square within line of sight with an imbued arrow or bolt, and uses that square as the center of the spell-like ability's area of effect on a hit (AC 5)." special="The arrow must be fired during the round it was imbued, or the spell-like ability is wasted. If the arrow or bolt misses, the use of the spell-like ability is wasted." replace="The 1st-level fighter bonus feat"}

::ab[Silent Shooter (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A cavern sniper gains a +2 bonus on Stealth checks made when loading a bow or crossbow, poisoning ammunition, and making sniping attempts." increment="This bonus~2~4~4~2" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Quick and Deadly (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power order=aw ability="The cavern sniper can move at full speed while using Stealth at no penalty." swift="He can apply poison to a single arrow or crossbow bolt." replace="The 4th-level fighter bonus feat"}

::ab[Sniper Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost choice="The cavern sniper chooses the bow or crossbow ‹ability/weapon group<s› and gains a +2 bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls." increment="This bonus~5~4" replace="Weapon training 1, 3, and 4"}

::ab[Greater Imbued Shot (Su)]{l=9 icon=power ability="The cavern sniper gains two extra uses of both his *faerie fire* and *darkness* spell-like abilities, but can only use these extra uses to imbue arrows and bolts with the *imbued shot* class feature." replace="Weapon training 2"}
::ab[Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{l=20 icon=info choice="When the cavern sniper gains *weapon mastery,* he must choose a weapon in either the bow or crossbow weapon group." alter="Weapon mastery"}

child_of_acavna_and_amaznen.name: Child of Acavna and Amaznen
description: ## Child of Acavna and Amaznen
‹SOURCE Arcane Anthology/10›

The child of ‹faith/Acavna› and ‹faith/Amaznen› has trained in the nearly forgotten arts of Azlant passed down by Aroden, which combine martial prowess with elementary wizardry. Inspired by the deities ‹faith/Aroden› once worshiped long ago, children of Acavna and Amaznen strive to understand the dangers of the world and overcome them with knowledge and strength of arms.
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down info="A child of Acavna and Amaznen is not proficient with two-handed martial weapons or tower shields." alter="Weapon and armor proficiency"}

::ab[Eldritch Lore]{icon=skill info="A child of Acavna and Amaznen gains 4 skill ranks + a number of skill ranks equal to her Intelligence modifier at each level, instead of the normal 2 skill ranks + her Intelligence modifier at each level. Furthermore, ‹skill/Knowledge› (arcana, religion) (INT), ‹skill/Linguistics› (INT), and ‹skill/Spellcraft› (INT) are all class skills for the child of Acavna and Amaznen." alter=Skills replace="Bonus fighter combat feat gained at 1st level"}

:::ab{l=2 title="Lore of Acavna and Amaznen (Ex)" icon=learn-boost action=Ability order=Cp passive="She also gains a +2 bonus on Knowledge checks relating to Azlant and the Azlanti, and on Knowledge checks to identify and learn the powers and weaknesses of aboleths, demons, serpentfolk, and undead." replace="Bonus feat gained at 2nd level"}

A child of Acavna and Amaznen is further initiated into the arcane secrets passed down from Azlant. This gives her minor spellcasting abilities and access to lore collected to give her an edge against the enemies of humanity.

The child of Acavna and Amaznen gains a spellbook containing four 0-level ‹main/wizard spell«s› of her choice. She can prepare and cast these spells as a ‹class/wizard›, using her fighter level as her caster level. She can prepare a number of 0-level spells each day equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-sixth› of her fighter level (see the table below).

::ab[Eldritch Armor Training (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def order=aw ability="A child of Acavna and Amaznen gains eldritch armor training. This functions as the normal *armor training* fighter ability." swift="She can reduce the arcane spell failure chance due to armor she is wearing by 15% for any spells she casts this round." imp7="This reduction increases to 20%." imp11="It increases to 25%." imp15="The reduction increases to 30%." special="In addition, if she has ‹feat/Arcane Armor Training› or ‹feat/Arcane Armor Mastery›, rather than having their normal effects, each of those feats increases the arcane spell failure reduction of her eldritch armor training by 10%." alter="Armor training"}

:::ab{l=5 title=Spells icon=learn action=ability special="Each time she attains a new fighter level, she gains one bloodrager spell of her choice to add to her spellbook. The free spells must be of spell levels she can cast. She otherwise follows the rules for a wizard regarding her spellbook and learning or preparing spells." replace="Weapon training~bonus feats gained at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 20th level"}

The spellcasting ability of a child of Acavna and Amaznen increases to allow her to cast a limited number of spells drawn from the ‹main/bloodrager spell« list›. She automatically adds to her spellbook a number of 1st-level bloodrager spells equal to 3 + her Intelligence modifier. Her caster level for these spells is equal to her fighter level, and the bloodrager class spell list is considered to be her class spell list for the purposes of meeting prerequisites and using magic items.

She uses the ‹class/ranger«'s› spells per day (see below), but uses her Intelligence score to determine her ‹rule/bonus spells› and spell saving throw DCs, just as a wizard does. She does not gain any other wizard abilities, such as an arcane bond or school.
| Level | 0 | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th |

corsair.name: Corsair
description: ## Corsair
‹SOURCE Pirates of the Inner Sea/22›

A corsair is a pirate who focuses on shipboard combat, relying on his strength of arms over his agility. He specializes in traditional pirate weapons, but often wears heavier armor than is normally seen on a ship. A corsair moves and fights easily in his armor, however, even in the water. Captains value corsairs because of the skill they exhibit in defending the ship and in boarding actions.

::ab[Pirate Weapons (Ex)]{icon=power flavor="A corsair is well versed in the types of weapons favored by pirates." choice="When selecting a ‹ability/group of »weapon«s<_groups› for his Weapon Training ability, he can select **Pirate Weapons** as a group. The Pirate Weapons group consists of the ‹eq-weapon/crossbow› (all types), ‹eq-weapon/cutlass›, ‹eq-weapon/dagger›, ‹eq-weapon/hook hand›, ‹eq-weapon/rapier›, and ‹eq-weapon/short sword›."}

::ab[Deck Fighting (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power-def flavor="A corsair is used to fighting on crowded decks, and isn't fazed by fighting multiple opponents at once." ability="He gains ‹feat/Cleave› as a bonus feat. He may use this feat even if he does not meet the prerequisites. A corsair does not take the normal -2 penalty to his AC when using the Cleave feat." replace="The fighter's 2nd-level bonus feat"}

::ab[Armored Pirate (Ex)]{flavor="A corsair wears heavier armor than is common aboard a ship." icon=boost l3="The corsair reduces the armor check penalty of any light armor he wears to 0 for purposes of Acrobatics and Swim checks." l7="This becomes true of medium armor as well." l11="This now includes heavy armor." replace="Armor training"}

::ab[Improved Deck Fighting (Ex)]{l=6 icon=power-def ability="The corsair gains ‹feat/Great Cleave› as a bonus feat. He may use this feat even if he does not meet the prerequisites. A corsair does not take the normal -2 penalty to his AC when using the Great Cleave feat." replace="The fighter's 6th-level bonus feat"}

crossbowman.name: Crossbowman
description: ## Crossbowman
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/104›
The crossbowman has perfected the deadly use of the ‹eq-weapon/crossbow›, a simple but cruelly efficient weapon, as a craftsman mastering a lethal tool.

::ab[Deadshot (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="When a crossbowman attacks with a crossbow as a ‹rule/readied action›, he may add ‹misc/half› his Dexterity bonus (minimum +1) on his damage roll." replace="Armor training 1"}
::ab[Crossbow Expert (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="A crossbowman gains a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls with crossbows." increment="This bonus~5~4" replace="Weapon training 1"}
::ab[Improved Deadshot (Ex)]{l=7 icon=lower passive="When a crossbowman attacks with a crossbow as a readied action, his target is denied its Dexterity bonus to its AC." replace="Armor training 2"}

::ab[Quick Sniper (Ex)]{l=9 icon=boost passive="A crossbowman gains a bonus equal to 1/2 his fighter level on Stealth checks when ‹skill/sniping›." order=pi immediate="When he is hit with a ranged attack, he can shoot his crossbow at his attacker if it is loaded." replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Greater Deadshot (Ex)]{l=11 icon=boost ability="When a crossbowman attacks with a crossbow as a readied action, he may add his Dexterity bonus (minimum +1) on his damage roll." replace="Armor training 3"}
::ab[Safe Shot (Ex)]{l=13 icon=def passive="A crossbowman does not provoke attacks of opportunity when making ranged attacks with a crossbow." replace="Weapon training 3"}
::ab[Pinpoint Targeting]{l=15 icon=power ability="A crossbowman gains ‹feat/Pinpoint Targeting› as a bonus feat." replace="Armor training 4"}

::ab[Meteor Shot (Ex)]{l=17 icon=ranged standard="A crossbowman can make one attack with a crossbow at a -4 penalty. If the attack hits, it inflicts damage normally and the target is subject to a ‹rule/bull rush› or a ‹rule/trip› maneuver using the attack roll as the combat maneuver check. The crossbowman must decide which maneuver to attempt before making his attack roll." replace="Weapon training 4"}

::ab[Penetrating Shot (Ex)]{l=19 icon=boost passive="When a crossbowman confirms a critical hit with a crossbow, the bolt pierces the target and can strike another creature in line behind it. The crossbowman must be able to trace a line starting at his space and passing through both targets to make this additional attack. The secondary attack is made at a -4 penalty, in addition to any modifiers for added range. If this attack is also a critical hit, the bolt can continue to hit another target, but the penalties stack." replace="Armor mastery"}
::ab[Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{icon=info info="A crossbowman must choose a type of crossbow when he receives *weapon mastery.*" alter="Weapon mastery"}

cyber_soldier.name: Cyber-Soldier
description: ## Cyber-Soldier
‹SOURCE Technology Guide/12›

Cyber-soldiers replace fallible flesh with precision-crafted machinery. Some seek to exceed the limitations of humanoid flesh, while others become cyber-soldiers through drastic repairs necessitated by horrific injuries. A cyber-soldier has the following class features.

::ab[Cybernetic Combat (Ex)]{l=5 icon=melee-boost order=ap passive="A cyber-soldier gains a +1 bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls with ‹tech-cyber/implanted weapons›, ‹tech-cyber/cybernetic arm«s›, and melee or thrown weapons wielded with a *cybernetic arm.* Weapons wielded with two *cybernetic arms* receive double the bonus, except when such weapons are thrown. This attack bonus does not stack with the attack bonus for weapon training." increment="The damage bonus~5~4" ability="A cyber-soldier gains a slam attack for each *cybernetic arm.* These attacks deal 1d6 points of bludgeoning damage for a Medium cyber-soldier and 1d4 points of bludgeoning damage for a Small cyber-soldier." replace="Weapon training 1"}

::ab[Improved Implantation (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost info="A cyber-soldier can exceed the normal limitations on ‹rule/cybertech› implantation. Her brain and body slots each accommodate one more piece of cybertech than normal. In addition, her limit on implantation points increases by ‹misc/half› her cyber-soldier level. She can allocate these points between her Intelligence and Constitution scores as she chooses." imp15="Her body slot can accommodate two more pieces of cybertech than normal." replace="Armor training 2 and 4"}

::ab[Resilience (Ex)]{l=19 icon=def passive="A cyber-soldier has a reservoir of 5 ‹rule/temporary hit points› for each piece of implanted cybertech in her body. Lost temporary hit points are recovered at the rate of 10 points per hour." replace="Armor mastery"}

dirty_fighter.name: Dirty Fighter
description: ## Dirty Fighter
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/140›

The dirty fighter laughs at concepts like honor and fair play. He cares only for victory, no matter how he achieves it, and spends as much time mastering sneaky combat maneuvers as he does drilling with weapons or learning how to wear armor. A dirty fighter has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Orc}

::ab[Sidestep (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def flavor="A dirty fighter learns how to evade his enemies when they react to his combat maneuvers." passive="He gains a +1 dodge bonus to his AC against attacks of opportunity provoked by him while attempting a combat maneuver." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Maneuver Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost-def flavor="A dirty fighter becomes a master of dirty tricks." passive="He gains a +2 bonus on ‹rule/dirty trick› combat maneuver checks and +2 to his CMD when he is the target of a dirty trick combat maneuver." replace="Weapon training 1"}

::ab[Speedy Tricks (Ex)]{l=9 icon=melee flavor="A dirty fighter has perfected how to quickly perform dirty tricks." ability="He can make a dirty trick combat maneuver as an attack instead of a standard action." replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Double Tricks (Ex)]{l=13 icon=boost ability="When a dirty fighter performs a combat maneuver, he may apply two different conditions to his target instead of one. Each penalty condition requires a separate action to remove." imp17="He may apply three different conditions." replace="Weapon training 3 and 4"}

dragonheir_scion.name: Dragonheir Scion
description: ## Dragonheir Scion
‹SOURCE Legacy of Dragons/12›

Dragonheir scions are the martially inclined humanoid descendants of those influenced by draconic power. Unlike their sorcerous brethren, dragonheir scions manifest their heritage in ways more suited to strength of arms and skill with steel than arcane energies. Those who follow this path are often the children of mighty dragon-blooded sorcerers and others who drew energy from their dragon blood, though they themselves might not exhibit spellcasting ability.
::cskill{gain=ka~umd lose=ha~ride}

:::ab{title="Draconic Bloodline (Su)" icon=power l=1 action=Choice flavor="Each dragonheir scion can draw her lineage back to the influence of a great draconic progenitor." special="If a dragonheir scion takes a level in another class that grants a bloodline, the bloodlines must be of the same type, even if that means that the bloodline of one of the classes must change. Subject to GM discretion, the dragonheir scion can change her former bloodline to make them conform."}

A dragonheir scion must select one of the chromatic or metallic dragon types. Once chosen, this cannot be changed. A number of dragonheir scion's abilities deal damage and grant resistances based on her dragon type, as noted below.
| Chromatic Dragon Type | Energy Type |
| Black | Acid |
| Blue | Electricity |
| Green | Acid |
| Red | Fire |
| White | Cold |
| Metallic Dragon Type | Energy Type |
| Brass | Fire |
| Bronze | Electricity |
| Copper | Acid |
| Gold | Fire |
| Silver | Cold |

::ab[Eldritch Strikes (Su)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A dragonheir scion gains ‹feat/Arcane Strike› as a bonus feat. She does not need to meet its prerequisites." replace="The bonus feat gained at 1st level"}

::ab[Fearful Might (Ex)]{icon=boost l1="A dragonheir scion gains a +1 bonus on Intimidate checks to demoralize a foe." l6="This bonus increases to +2." l10="This bonus becomes +3." l14="This bonus increases to +4." l18="This bonus becomes +5." replace=Bravery}

::ab[Draconic Defense (Su)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A dragonheir scion gains a +1 natural armor bonus and energy resistance 5 against her energy type." imp7="This increases to a +2 natural armor bonus and energy resistance 10." imp13="It increases to a +3 natural armor bonus and energy resistance 20." replace="Armor training 1, 2, and 3"}

::ab[Draconic Strike (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost passive="Whenever the dragonheir scion damages a target with an attack augmented by Arcane Strike, the attack deals an additional 1d4 points of damage of the dragonheir scion's energy type." replace="Bonus feat gained at 4th level.@FN1"}

::ab[Draconic Presense (Su)]{l=6 icon=power ability="The dragonheir scion receives ‹feat/Dazzling Display› as a bonus feat. She does not need a weapon in hand to use this ability, and can use it as a @HLstandard_action." replace="Bonus feat gained at 6th level.@FN1-2"}

::ab[Wings (Su)]{l=15 icon=power standard="A dragonheir scion can grow leathery dragon wings from her back, granting her a fly speed of 60 feet with average maneuverability. She can dismiss the wings as a @HLfree_action." replace="Armor training 4~armor mastery"}

::ab[Might of Wyrms (Su)]{l=20 icon=def-power passive="A dragonheir scion's draconic heritage becomes manifest. She gains ‹umr/immunity› to paralysis, sleep, and damage of her energy type, as well as ‹umr/blindsense› with a range of 60 feet." replace="Weapon mastery"}
:::fakeFootnotes

@FN[The original text says that *Draconic Strike* is a 3rd-level ability that replaces your 3rd-level bonus feat, and *Draconic Presense* likewise targets your 5th level. However, fighters do not get bonus feats at 3rd or 5th level. The general concensus is that they should be bumped to 4th and 6th level respectively, which is what is shown above.]{from=1 multi=2}

dragoon.name: Dragoon
description: ## Dragoon
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/46›
These gallant lancers serve in the vanguard of many armies or as knights-errant. They are born leaders and masters of the mounted charge.
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down info="A dragoon is not proficient with tower shields."}
::ab[Skilled Rider (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A dragoon gains both the ‹feat/Mounted Combat› and ‹feat/Skill Focus› (Ride) feats as bonus feats." replace="The 1st-level fighter bonus combat feat"}

::ab[Spear Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=info choice="A dragoon must select *weapon training* with the ‹ability/weapon>spear» group<s›." imp9="The dragoon's *weapon training* bonus with spears improves by +1 on attack rolls and +2 on damage rolls." imp13="The dragoon's *weapon training* bonus with spears improves by another +1 on attack rolls and another +2 on damage rolls." imp17="The dragoon's *weapon training* bonus with spears improves by yet another +1 on attack rolls and +2 on damage rolls." special="The dragoon does not gain *weapon training* in any other groups as he increases in level." alter="Weapon training"}

::ab[Spinning Lance (Ex)]{l=7 icon=melee ability="A dragoon may alternate attacks with the piercing head of his ‹eq-weapon/lance› with reach, or with the butt end (treat as a ‹eq-weapon/club›) against adjacent targets." special="Unlike a double weapon, the masterwork quality and magical special abilities apply to both ends of the lance, except for those weapon special abilities that apply only to edged weapons." replace="Armor training 2"}

::ab[Banner (Ex)]{l=9 icon=power ability="A dragoon may attach a banner to his lance. This is identical to the ‹class/cavalier› class feature." special="Cavalier levels stack with his dragoon level for determining the effect of his banner, and he can take the better banner progression." replace="Weapon training 2, 3, and 4" imp14="The bonuses provided by the dragoon's banner increase by +1." imp19="The bonuses provided by the dragoon's banner increase by +2."}

::ab[Piercing Lance (Ex)]{l=11 icon=melee-boost ability="As a @HLstandard_action or as part of a ‹rule/charge›, a dragoon attacking a mounted opponent can make two attacks, one against the mount and the other against the rider, using his highest base attack bonus. Furthermore, if the mount is hit and its rider attempts to negate the hit with the Mounted Combat feat, the dragoon's attack roll is considered 4 higher when calculating the DC of the Ride check to negate the hit." replace="Armor training 3"}

::ab[Leaping Lance (Ex)]{l=15 icon=boost passive="A dragoon and his mount suffer no armor check penalty on Acrobatics checks while mounted." order=pa ability="When charging, a dragoon may jump from his mount toward his target. If he jumps 10 feet, his charge modifiers on attack rolls and to AC are doubled and he is still considered mounted for lance damage, mounted combat feats, and so on." replace="Armor training 4" special="This ability qualifies you for the ‹feat/Rebounding Leap› feat."}
::ab[Weapon Mastery]{icon=info choice="The dragoon must select the lance for *weapon mastery.*" alter="Weapon mastery"}

drill_sergeant.name: Drill Sergeant
description: ## Drill Sergeant
‹SOURCE Melee Tactics Toolbox/11›
Drill sergeants excel at training other combatants in fighting techniques.

::ab[Tactician (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A drill sergeant gains the ‹class/cavalier«'s› *tactician* class feature, treating his fighter level as his cavalier level for the purposes of this ability." replace=Bravery head}

::ab[Tactician (Ex)]{flavor="Text from the cavalier class" next icon=power-boost order=asu ability="You receive a ‹main/teamwork feat› as a bonus feat. You must meet the prerequisites for this feat." standard="You can grant this feat to all allies within 30 feet who can see and hear you. Allies retain the use of this bonus feat for 3 rounds plus 1 round for every two levels you possess. Allies do not need to meet the prerequisites of these bonus feats." useInc=fighter~5~+ useF="2~1~5~~5"}

::ab[Weapon Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=down ability="A drill sergeant gains *weapon training* with one ‹ability/group of »weapon«s<_groups›. He doesn't gain *weapon training* with other groups at later levels, though his *weapon training* bonus improves by 1 for every 4 levels after 5th level." alter="Weapon training 1"}
::ab[Greater Tactician (Ex)]{l=9 icon=power ability="The drill sergeant gains the cavalier's *greater tactician* class feature." replace="Weapon training 2" head}

::ab[Greater Tactician (Ex)]{flavor="Text from the cavalier class" next icon=power order=aw ability="You receive an additional teamwork feat as a bonus feat. You must meet the prerequisites for this feat. You can grant this feat to your allies using the *tactician* ability." swift="Using the *tactician* ability becomes a @HLswift_action."}

::ab[Weapon Trainer (Ex)]{l=13 icon=boost passive="When a drill sergeant wields a weapon he has *weapon training* in, all allies within 30 feet who can see and hear the drill sergeant gain ‹misc/half› his *weapon training* bonus when they wield any weapon from the same weapon group. This bonus doesn't stack with any *weapon training* bonus an ally already possesses." replace="Weapon training 3"}
::ab[Master Tactician (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power ability="The drill sergeant gains the cavalier's *master tactician* class feature." replace="Weapon training 4" head}

::ab[Master Tactician (Ex)]{flavor="Text from the cavalier ability" next icon=power ability="You receive an additional teamwork feat as a bonus feat. You must meet the prerequisites for this feat. You can grant this feat to your allies using the *tactician* ability." special="Whenever you use the *tactician* ability, you grant any two teamwork feats that you know. You can select from **any** of your teamwork feats, not just your bonus feats."}

druman_blackjacket.name: Druman Blackjacket
description: ## Druman Blackjacket
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/36›

Known as the Blackjackets, the elite soldiers of Druma are and always have been mercenaries rather than a standing army. Still, gold buys loyalty, and the Kalistocrats pay best of all. As a result, the Blackjackets are well equipped and dedicate themselves to training in small-unit tactics, quickly communicating strategies and tricks to handle any threat to their charges.

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=boost choice="In addition to combat feats, a Druman Blackjacket can choose ‹feat/Improved Iron Will›, ‹feat/Iron Will›, or any ‹main/teamwork feat› as bonus feats." alter="Bonus fighter feats"}

::ab[Well-Paid Loyalty (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A Druman Blackjacket gains a +1 bonus on Will saves against compulsion spells, spell-like abilities, and effects." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Blackjacket Tactics (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="Two or more Druman Blackjackets can spend 1 minute discussing strategy, then choose a single teamwork feat possessed by at least one of them. For a number of hours equal to the class level of the highest-level Blackjacket, the participants each gain the benefits of the selected feat even if they do not meet the feat's prerequisites. If a creature benefiting from this ability moves more than 30 feet away from any other participant, it loses all benefits of this ability until it is again within 30 feet." special="Creatures that do not have levels in this archetype can't benefit from this ability, and no creature can benefit from this ability more than once at any given time." replace="The bonus feat gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Amateurs! (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power-boost-def ability="When using the *Blackjacket tactics* ability, a Druman Blackjacket can include one or more creatures without levels in this archetype, but doing so reduces the duration of the effect to a number of minutes equal to the class level of the highest-level Blackjacket instead of a number of hours per level. If only Blackjackets are included in the effect, all Druman Blackjackets affected gain a +1 morale bonus on skill checks and saving throws for the duration of the effect." replace="The bonus feat gained at 8th level"}

::ab[Superior Tactics (Ex)]{l=12 icon=power ability="A Druman Blackjacket who uses the *Blackjacket tactics* ability can allow all affected Blackjackets to gain a second bonus teamwork feat possessed by at least one of the participants." replace="The bonus feat gained at 12th level"}

::ab[Esprit de Corps (Ex)]{l=16 icon=boost-def ability="A Druman Blackjacket grants all Blackjackets with whom he is sharing one or more teamwork feats through the *Blackjacket tactics* ability a +2 morale bonus on attack rolls, ability checks, skill checks, and saving throws. Each affected Blackjacket increases this bonus by 1 if another affected Blackjacket has been reduced to 0 or fewer hit points since the beginning of his last turn." replace="The bonus feat gained at 16th level"}

eldritch_guardian.name: Eldritch Guardian
description: ## Eldritch Guardian
‹SOURCE Familiar Folio/7›
Eldritch guardians are trained to detect and give warning about magic threats to the people and places they protect.
::cskill{gain=per~spc~umd lose=intm~ride~swim}

::ab[Familiar (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="An eldritch guardian gains a ‹sidekick/familiar›, treating his fighter level as his effective ‹class/wizard› level for the purpose of this ability." replace="The bonus feat gained at 1st level"}

::ab[Share Training (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="When the familiar can see and hear its master, it can use any combat feat possessed by the eldritch guardian. The familiar doesn't have to meet the feat's prerequisites, but at the GM's discretion may be precluded from using certain combat feats due to its physical form. For example, an eldritch guardian's pig familiar with access to ‹feat/Exotic Weapon Proficiency› (spiked chain) would not gain the ability to use spiked chains, since it doesn't have any limbs capable of properly handling them." replace="The bonus feat gained at 2nd level"}
::ab[Steel Will (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="The eldritch guardian gains a +1 bonus on Will saves against fear and mind-affecting effects." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

foehammer.name: Foehammer
description: ## Foehammer
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/14›
While the axe is the most famous dwarven weapon, the hammer is at the heart of dwarves' heritage as forgemasters and warriors alike.
::prereq{r=Dwarf}

::ab[Sledgehammer (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A foehammer wielding a hammer gains a +2 circumstance bonus on combat maneuver checks made to ‹rule/bull rush›, ‹rule/overrun›, ‹rule/sunder›, or ‹rule/trip›." replace="Armor training 1"}

::ab[Weapon Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=down passive="A foehammer must select hammers and does not gain weapon training with ‹ability/weapon>other» groups›, though his weapon training bonus improves by +1 every four levels after 5th."}

::ab[Hammer to the Ground (Ex)]{l=7 icon=melee ability="When a foehammer succeeds at a bull rush combat maneuver, he can make a trip combat maneuver at the end of the bull rush. If he does not move with the target, the force of his blow may still trip his foe, but he takes a -5 penalty on the combat maneuver check to trip." imp15="Any creature a foehammer successfully bull rushes is automatically knocked ‹rule/prone› at the end of the bull rush." replace="Armor training 2 and 4"}

::ab[Rhythmic Blows (Ex)]{l=9 icon=boost passive="Each time that a foehammer hits a target, he gains a +1 bonus on attack rolls against that target. This bonus stacks with each hit against that target, but lasts only until the end of the foehammer's turn." replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Piledriver (Ex)]{l=11 icon=melee standard="A foehammer may make a single melee attack with a weapon from the hammer weapon training group. If the attack hits, he may make a bull rush or trip combat maneuver against the target of his attack as a @HLfree_action that does not provoke an attack of opportunity." replace="Armor training 3"}

::ab[Ground Breaker (Ex)]{l=13 icon=power fullround="A foehammer may strike the ground with his hammer. If the attack deals more damage than the floor's hardness, the space he occupies and all adjacent squares become ‹rule/difficult terrain›. Creatures in those squares, except for the foehammer, are knocked prone (DC 15 Reflex negates)." replace="Weapon training 3"}

::ab[Hammer Master (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power ability="Any combat feats a foehammer has learned with any weapon from the hammer weapon training group (e.g., ‹feat/Improved Critical›, ‹feat/Weapon Focus›) apply to all weapons from that group." replace="Weapon training 4"}

::ab[Devastating Blow (Ex)]{l=19 icon=melee standard="A foehammer may make a single melee attack with a weapon from the hammer weapon training group at a -5 penalty. If the attack hits, it is treated as a critical threat. Weapon special abilities that only activate on a critical hit do not activate if this critical hit is confirmed." replace="Armor mastery"}
::ab[Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{icon=info info="A foehammer must choose a weapon from the hammer group." alter="Weapon mastery"}

free_hand_fighter.name: Free Hand Fighter
description: ## Free Hand Fighter
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/105›

The free hand fighter specializes in the delicate art of handling a single weapon in one hand while using his free hand to balance, block, tip, and distract his opponents. While not a brawler, his open hand is as much a weapon as a bow or blade. His fighting school benefits only apply when he is using a one-handed weapon and carrying nothing in his other hand.

::ab[Deceptive Strike (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A free hand fighter gains a +1 bonus to CMB and CMD on ‹rule/disarm› checks and on Bluff checks to feint or create a diversion to hide." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Elusive (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A free hand fighter gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC." special="This bonus does not apply when wearing medium or heavy armor or carrying a medium or heavier load." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace="Armor training 1, 2, 3, and 4"}

::ab[Singleton (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="A free hand fighter gains a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls when wielding a melee weapon in one hand and leaving his other hand free." increment="This bonus~5~6" replace="Weapon training 1 and 4"}

::ab[Timely Tip (Ex)]{l=9 icon=melee-lower move="A free hand fighter can make a disarm combat maneuver against a target he threatens to push aside the target's shield. If successful, the target loses its shield bonus to AC against the free hand fighter's next attack." replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Interference (Ex)]{l=13 icon=melee-lower move="A free hand fighter can make a disarm or ‹rule/trip› combat maneuver against a target he threatens to push his opponent off balance. If successful, the target becomes ‹rule/flat-footed›. This condition lasts until the target takes damage from a melee or ranged attack or until the beginning of the free hand fighter's next turn, whichever comes first." replace="Weapon training 3"}

::ab[Reversal (Ex)]{l=19 icon=melee-def immediate="A free hand fighter can make a disarm combat maneuver against a creature he threatens when he is the target of a melee attack from another creature. If successful, the attack changes to target the target of the free hand fighter's maneuver instead of the free hand fighter himself." replace="Armor mastery"}

gladiator.name: Gladiator
description: ## Gladiator
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/47›

Most fighters battle out of necessity, with each battle a contest of life and death. Some, however, fight for glory and for the adulation of the crowd. The gladiator is both a cunning warrior and a consummate performer, knowing life and death are balanced not only on a sword's edge, but also on the cheers or jeers of the crowd.
::cskill{gain="‹skill/Perform« (act, comedy, and dance)› (CHA)"}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-def ability="A gladiator gains the ‹feat/Performance Weapon Mastery› feat at 1st level as a bonus feat. This bonus feat is in addition to the bonus feat a fighter gains at 1st level. Furthermore, a gladiator using ‹rule/piecemeal armor› is considered to be wearing a suit of armor as long as he wearing two or three armor pieces, gaining the +1 to armor bonus but still taking the +5% chance of arcane spell failure if the pieces are mixed." replace="Proficiency with heavy armor and tower shields"}
::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power ability="A gladiator may choose to take ‹main/combat<_feat› or ‹main/feats_>performance› feats as bonus feats."}

::ab[Fame (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="When a gladiator begins a ‹rule/performance combat›, he always starts with at least 1 victory point. If he already has victory points, he gains 1 extra victory point." imp10="The gladiator starts out with at least 2 victory points. If he already has victory points, he gains 2 extra victory points." replace=Bravery}

gloomblade.name: Gloomblade
description: ## Gloomblade
‹SOURCE Planar Adventures/14›
The Shadow Plane's substance is legendary for its versatility. In shadow-shrouded lands, secretive martial practitioners long ago learned to shape supernatural weapons from ribbons of pure darkness.
::cskill{gain=acro~kp~per~stl lose=climb~ha~ride~swim}

::ab[Shadow Weapon (Su)]{icon=power-magic move="A gloomblade can create a shadowy weapon in a free hand. This can take the form of any melee weapon with which he is proficient. A gloomblade can have only one shadow weapon in existence at a time; creating a new shadow weapon causes an existing shadow weapon to vanish." imp3="The shadow weapon acts as a magic weapon with a +1 enhancement bonus." increment="The weapon's enhancement bonus~2~4" imp7="A gloomblade can create (and maintain) two shadow weapons at a time as a @HLmove_action; if he does, each weapon has an enhancement bonus 1 lower than normal. If a gloomblade creates only one weapon, it gains a weapon special ability of his choice (chosen upon creation); the ability must be valid for the shadow weapon's weapon type and must be chosen from ‹magic-enh/defending›, ‹magic-enh/flaming›, ‹magic-enh/frost›, ‹magic-enh/keen›, ‹magic-enh/ghost touch›, ‹magic-enh/merciful›, ‹magic-enh/shock›, ‹magic-enh/thundering›, or ‹magic-enh/vicious›. (Additional special abilities might qualify, at the GM's discretion.)" imp11="A gloomblade's shadow weapons each gain their full enhancement bonus if he creates two weapons. If a gloomblade creates only one shadow weapon, it gains additional weapon special abilities; the total effective bonus of these abilities cannot exceed +3. The gloomblade can now choose from the of ‹magic-enh/anarchic›, ‹magic-enh/axiomatic›, ‹magic-enh/flaming burst›, ‹magic-enh/icy burst›, ‹magic-enh/holy›, ‹magic-enh/shocking burst›, ‹magic-enh/unholy›, and ‹magic-enh/wounding› weapon special abilities as well as those from the list above." imp15="A gloomblade's shadow weapons each gain magic weapon special abilities with a total effective bonus of +2 per weapon. If a gloomblade creates only one shadow weapon, its magic weapon special abilities cannot exceed a total effective bonus of +5. The gloomblade adds ‹magic-enh/brilliant energy›, ‹magic-enh/dancing›, and ‹magic-enh/speed› to the options he can choose as weapon special abilities for his shadow weapon." replace="Armor training~proficiency with heavy armor and shields" special="He retains proficiency in light and medium armor"}

:::ab{l=5 title="Shadow Weapon Training (Ex)" action="At 9th Level" icon=down ability="A gloomblade gains weapon training, except that he does not select a weapon group; instead, the bonus applies to the shadow weapons he creates. A gloomblade does not select additional weapon groups as he gains levels." alter="Weapon training" order=aC imp13="The gloomblade can apply two effects to his shadow weapons. These effects can be split between multiple shadow weapons as a gloomblade desires, but they don't stack with themselves." imp17="The gloomblade can apply three effects to his shadow weapons. These effects can be split between multiple shadow weapons as a gloomblade desires, but they don't stack with themselves."}
Whenever a gloomblade creates one or more shadow weapons, he can apply any one of the following shadow effects (chosen upon creation) to one of them.
- During the gloomblade's turn, he can increase his reach with that weapon by 5 feet.
- The gloomblade can increase a thrown shadow weapon's range increment (if any) by 20 feet.

- When he hits a foe with a melee attack with the weapon, he can use it to perform a ‹rule/reposition› combat maneuver against the creature hit as a @HL[swift action]. This does not provoke attacks of opportunity.
- Attacks made with the weapon ignore hardness equal to or less than the gloomblade's fighter level.

high_guardian.name: High Guardian
description: ## High Guardian
‹SOURCE Heroes of the High Court/8›

As the shield that protects his lord from the myriad dangers around every corner, the high guardian pledges his life to keep his charge safe. High guardians epitomize personal devotion, making them especially popular among Taldan nobles envious of the emperor's ‹rule/Ulfen Guard›, as well as in the courts of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, the war councils of Molthune and Lastwall, and the refined chambers of Osirion.
::cskill{gain=kl~kno lose=kd~ke}

::ab[Obligation (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power-protect ability="A high guardian can spend 1 minute of focused concentration each day to select a single ally as his *obligation,* vowing to keep that person alive for that day. Once he has chosen, he can't change his *obligation* until the following day. If his *obligation* dies, the high guardian must atone for 1 week before he can select another *obligation.*"}

::ab[Right Hand (Ex)]{l=1 icon=protect immediate="A high guardian can take ‹rule/a 5-foot step›, as long as he ends this movement adjacent to his *obligation.* If he takes this step, he cannot take a 5-foot step during his next turn and his total movement is reduced by 5 feet during his next turn." replace="The bonus feat gained at 1st level"}

::ab[Defender's Reflexes (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A high guardian gains ‹feat/Combat Reflexes› as a bonus feat, and he can use his Strength modifier instead of his Dexterity modifier to determine the number of additional attacks of opportunity he can make per round. If he already has Combat Reflexes, he instead gains ‹feat/Stand Still› as a bonus feat." replace="The bonus feat gained at 2nd level"}
::ab[Unassailable Allegiance (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A high guardian gains a +1 bonus on Will saves against compulsion spells and effects." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Royal Protector (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A high guardian gains ‹feat/Bodyguard› and ‹feat/In Harm's Way› as bonus feats, though he can use them only to improve his *obligation's* AC or intercept a successful attack against his *obligation.*" replace="The bonus feat gained at 4th level"}

lore_warden.name: Lore Warden
description: ## Lore Warden
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/144›

Quick wits and deceptive techniques can often succeed where brute force might not. A lore warden is the consummate warrior-scholar of the ‹rule/Pathfinder Society›, outsmarting her competition even when locking blades with powerful foes. Outside of combat, a lore warden's extensive education also helps her document the past and survive great danger.

::ab[Scholastic (Ex)]{icon=skill info="A lore warden gains 2 additional skill ranks each level. These ranks must be spent on Intelligence-based skills. All ‹skill/Craft› (CHA) and ‹skill/Knowledge› (INT) skills are class skills for lore wardens, as are ‹skill/Linguistics› (INT) and ‹skill/Spellcraft› (INT)." replace="Proficiency with medium armor, heavy armor, and shields"}

::ab[Skill Over Strength (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power passive="A lore warden qualifies for feats and other abilities as though she had the ‹feat/Combat Expertise› feat." imp6="Se gains Combat Expertise as a bonus feat, even if she would not normally qualify for the feat. If she already has Combat Expertise, she instead gains any one combat feat that includes Combat Expertise as a prerequisite (and for which she otherwise qualifies)." imp10="She can treat her base attack bonus as though it were 2 higher for the purpose of calculating the effects of Combat Expertise." replace="Bravery~fighter bonus feat gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Swords Secret (Ex)]{head l=3 icon=power flavor="A lore warden learns specialized techniques that help her to quickly analyze and defeat her foes." choice="A lore warden gains one swords secret. Except where noted, a lore warden cannot select the same swords secret more than once." imp7="She chooses a second swords secret." imp11="She chooses a third swords secret." imp15="She chooses a fourth swords secret." imp19="She chooses a fifth swords secret."}

::ab[Exploit Weakness (Ex)]{next icon=boost passive="The lore warden adds ‹misc/one-third› her class level on attack rolls to confirm critical hits." imp11="Whenever she confirms a critical hit, her weapon attacks ignore the first 5 points of damage reduction or hardness the target has until the end of her next turn." imp19="The lore warden can automatically confirm a critical hit once per round when she threatens a critical hit."}

::ab[Hair's Breadth (Ex)]{next prereq="11th-level lore warden" icon=def immediate="Once per day when subject to a critical hit, the lore warden can attempt an Acrobatics check to reduce the damage. If the result of this Acrobatics check is greater than the opponent's confirmation roll, she negates the critical hit; the attack still hits and deals normal damage." imp15="She can use this secret one additional time per day."}

::ab[Know Thy Enemy (Ex)]{next icon=boost standard="When the lore warden succeeds at a Knowledge check to identify a creature's abilities and weaknesses, she can also use a @HLstandard_action to grant herself a +2 insight bonus on all attack and weapon damage rolls made against that enemy. This bonus lasts for a number of rounds equal to half her class level (minimum 2 rounds), or until the lore warden uses this ability against a different creature." imp11="She also gains a +2 bonus to her AC against the creature when using this ability." imp19="The insight bonus increases to +3."}

::ab[Swift Assessment (Ex)]{next prereq="Know Thy Enemy" icon=boost ability="The lore warden can now use her *know thy enemy* swords secret as a @HLmove_action." imp15="She can use this ability as a @HLswift_action."}

::ab[Maneuver Training (Ex)]{next icon=power ability="The lore warden gains a brawler's *maneuver training* class feature, treating her fighter level as her ‹class/brawler› level." replace="Armor training~armor mastery"}

lore_warden_pfs_field_guide.name: Lore Warden (PFS Field Guide)
description: ## Lore Warden (PFS Field Guide)
‹SOURCE Pathfinder Society Field Guide/31›

Quick thinking and deception can often carry the day where raw force might not. Lore wardens are the bodyguards, guardians, and soldiers of the ‹rule/Pathfinder Society›. Not only are they often called upon to protect and watch over important repositories of lore, but they themselves are impressive keepers of all manner of knowledge. Lore wardens are fighters who benefit from learning to outsmart and outmaneuver their foes rather than just overpower them. Even out of combat, lore wardens have a quick wit and a surprising breadth of knowledge that can often grant them significant advantages over enemies even before a battle begins. Indeed, for many lore wardens, a fight that can be won without a single weapon being drawn is the best kind of victory. A lore warden has the following class features.

::ab[Scholastic (Ex)]{icon=skill info="Lore wardens gain 2 additional skill ranks each level. These ranks must be spent on Intelligence-based skills. All Intelligence-based skills are class skills for lore wardens." replace="Proficiency with medium armor, heavy armor, and shields"}
::ab[Expertise (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A lore warden gains ‹feat/Combat Expertise› as a bonus feat, even if he would not normally qualify for this feat." replace="Bravery 1"}
::ab[Maneuver Mastery (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost-def passive="A lore warden gains a +2 bonus on all CMB checks and to his CMD." incrementAt="This bonus~7~11~15~4/2" replace="Armor training 1"}

::ab[Know Thy Enemy (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost standard="A lore warden can study a specific target in sight. He must make a Knowledge check to determine the target's abilities and weaknesses as part of this standard action. If successful, the lore warden not only notes the appropriate abilities and weaknesses, as detailed under the Knowledge skill, but also gains a +2 competence bonus on all attack rolls and weapon damage rolls made against that enemy. This bonus lasts for the duration of an encounter, or until the lore warden attempts a new Knowledge check to use this ability on a different target." special="Note that this bonus on attack and damage rolls applies only to that specific creature." replace="Armor training 2"}

::ab[Hair's Breadth (Ex)]{l=11 icon=def immediate="A lore warden can attempt an Acrobatics check to negate a confirmation role for a critical hit. The critical hit confirmation is negated (though the attack still hits and deals normal damage) if the Acrobatics check is greater than the opponent's confirmation roll." replace="Armor training 3"}
::ab[Swift Lore (Ex)]{l=14 icon=boost ability="A lore warden can use his *Know Thy Enemy* ability as a @HLswift_action." replace="Armor training 4"}
::ab[Know Weakness (Ex)]{l=19 icon=boost ability="A lore warden can automatically confirm a critical hit once per round when he threatens a critical hit." replace="Armor mastery"}

martial_master.name: Martial Master
description: ## Martial Master
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/93›
There are those who learn the fighting arts though countless hours of repetition and training, while others seem to pick up new stances and forms as if they were born to them.

:::ab{l=5 title="Martial Flexibility (Ex)" icon=power useInc=fighter~2~+~3 useF="5~4~2~~6" action=Ability replace="Weapon training~weapon mastery" imp9="A martial master can use this ability to gain the benefit of two combat feats at the same time. He can select one feat as a @HLswift_action or two feats as a @HLmove_action." imp14="A martial master can use this ability to gain the benefit of three combat feats at the same time. He can select one feat as a @HLfree_action, two feats as a @HLswift_action, or three feats as a @HLmove_action." imp17="A martial master can use this ability to gain the benefit of one combat feat as an @HLimmediate_action or three combat feats as a @HLswift_action." imp20="A martial master can use this ability to gain the benefit of any number of combat feats as a @HLswift_action."}

The martial master can use a @HLmove_action to gain the benefit of a combat feat he doesn't possess. This effect lasts for 1 minute. The martial master must otherwise meet all the feat's prerequisites.

The martial master can use this ability again before the duration expires in order to replace the previous combat feat with another choice. If a combat feat has a daily use limitation (such as ‹feat/Stunning Fist›), any uses of that combat feat while using this ability count toward that feat's daily limit. At later levels, when he gains multiple feats through this ability, the martial master can use those feats to meet the prerequisites of other feats he gains with this ability. Doing so means he cannot replace a feat currently fulfilling another's prerequisite without also replacing those feats that require it. Each individual feat selected counts toward his daily uses of this ability.

mobile_fighter.name: Mobile Fighter
description: ## Mobile Fighter
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/105›
Where some fighters focus on strength and raw power, the mobile fighter relies on swiftness and mobility, gliding across the battlefield like a steel whirlwind and leaving destruction in his wake.

::ab[Agility (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A mobile fighter gains a +1 bonus on saving throws made against effects that cause him to become ‹rule/paralyzed›, ‹spell/slow«ed›, or ‹rule/entangled›." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}
